courses.title: C Programming: System Architecture & Algorithms
courses.description: Master the foundational language of modern computer science. This course strips away high-level abstractions, teaching you how to directly manage memory, construct high-performance algorithms, and understand system architecture at its core.

modules.title: Module 1: Introduction to Compilation & Execution
modules.content: Understand the lifecycle of a C program. We explore the GCC compiler chain: Preprocessing, Compilation, Assembly, and Linking. Practical activity: Write your first 'Hello World' and manually inspect the assembly output file (.s) generated by the compiler.

modules.title: Module 2: Variables, Data Types & Memory Allocation
modules.content: An in-depth look at primitive data types (int, char, float, double). You will learn how the system assigns bytes to distinct primitives securely. Practical task: Utilize the 'sizeof' operator to map the byte structure of your local machine's architecture.

modules.title: Module 3: Control Flow & Logical Structures
modules.content: Master runtime decision making using if/else statements, switch cases, and loops (for, while, do-while). Understand loop optimization and preventing infinite runtime locks. Practical activity: Build a secure interactive CLI menu.

modules.title: Module 4: Arrays & Dimensional Memory Management
modules.content: Understand contiguous runtime memory allocation. Explore single and multi-dimensional arrays, defining boundary constraints securely, and the risks of buffer over-reads. Practical: Construct a matrix multiplication algorithm.

modules.title: Module 5: Functions, Scope & The Call Stack
modules.content: Learn how functions govern modularity. We dissect the Call Stack, exploring push/pop behaviors, variable scope (local vs global), and stack overflow vulnerabilities. Practical: Develop a functional library for statistical mathematics.

modules.title: Module 6: Pointers & Direct Memory Access
modules.content: The most critical concept in C. Master pointer arithmetic, the reference (&) and dereference (*) operators, and how pointers pass arguments by reference effectively minimizing memory overhead.

modules.title: Module 7: Dynamic Memory Allocation (Heap)
modules.content: Break away from the fixed-size Stack. Utilize malloc(), calloc(), realloc(), and free(). Practical activity: Develop a dynamic array wrapper that automatically resizes based on inputted payloads, strictly avoiding memory leaks.

modules.title: Module 8: Structs, Unions & Custom Data Models
modules.content: Combine distinct primitive types into complex structured blocks. Learn struct packing, byte alignment optimization, and typedef definitions. Practical: Build a modular Employee Database architecture.

modules.title: Module 9: File Handling & I/O Streams
modules.content: Persist data across executions. Understand file pointers (FILE *), and utilize fopen, fread, fwrite, and fclose securely. Practical task: Write a binary file parser that encrypts strings directly to disk.

modules.title: Module 10: Advanced Algorithms & Final Project
modules.content: Implement Linked Lists and Binary Search Trees utilizing your full knowledge of Pointers and Dynamic Allocation. Final execution: Build a highly optimized, fully persistent CLI Task Manager written entirely from scratch.

courses.title: Python Programming: Automation & Core Logic
courses.description: Python is the backbone of modern automation, AI, and rapid application deployment. This course rapidly transitions from syntax fundamentals into advanced procedural workflows and object-oriented architectures.

modules.title: Module 1: Syntax Constraints & Interpreters
modules.content: Configure your Python execution environment. Understand indentation logic, dynamic typing capabilities, and the difference between script execution and the interactive REPL. Practical: Construct a dynamic script utilizing sys.argv.

modules.title: Module 2: Primitives & Advanced String Formatting
modules.content: Master integers, floats, booleans, and strings. Explore deep string manipulation using f-strings, slicing, and native string methods to parse heavy data blocks effectively.

modules.title: Module 3: Core Data Structures (Lists & Tuples)
modules.content: Deploy dynamic Lists and immutable Tuples. Understand the 'List Comprehension' paradigm for rapid one-line iteration and logic injection. Practical: Filter out corrupted records from a raw data set dynamically.

modules.title: Module 4: Lookups & Mapping (Dictionaries & Sets)
modules.content: Harness hash-maps via Dictionaries for O(1) rapid lookups. Utilize Sets to execute massive mathematical operations natively (unions, intersections, differences). Practical: Build a scalable word-frequency analysis tool.

modules.title: Module 5: Workflow Control & Exception Handling
modules.content: Implement structural if/else trees and iterative loops natively. Deep-dive into robust Error catchments utilizing try/except/finally blocks to prevent crashing during faulty I/O execution.

modules.title: Module 6: Functions & High-Order Paradigms
modules.content: Deploy cleanly scoped functions, dynamic *args and **kwargs, and anonymous lambda expressions. Implement advanced conceptual mapping using native map() and filter() parameters.

modules.title: Module 7: Object-Oriented Execution (OOP)
modules.content: Understand Classes, Objects, and the `__init__` constructor methods natively. Learn encapsulation, static methods, and polymorphism scaling. Practical: Write an interconnected ATM banking application safely.

modules.title: Module 8: Module Management & Pip Ecosystem
modules.content: Escape native libraries and leverage `pip` package manager. Deploy virtual environments (venv) securely to prevent local dependency collisions. Practical: Isolate a project requesting dynamic API endpoints via the `requests` library.

modules.title: Module 9: File I/O & JSON Parsing
modules.content: Learn robust context managers (`with open()`) ensuring native file closure. Perform seamless reading/writing across CSV and serialized JSON frameworks natively.

modules.title: Module 10: Final Deployment Project
modules.content: Build an Automated Web Scraper. Leverage your understanding of dictionaries, error handling, lists, and external modules to securely parse, map, and output data directly into a sanitized CSV file.

courses.title: Java Programming: Enterprise Object-Oriented Principles
courses.description: Java operates the backbone of enterprise applications natively through the JVM. Learn strict, robust typing schemas, heavy Object-Oriented enforcement, and highly scalable memory garbage-collection mechanics.

modules.title: Module 1: The JVM & Execution Workflow
modules.content: Understand Java's 'Write Once, Run Anywhere' paradigm natively. Install the JDK, compile raw .java sources into bytecode (.class), and execute them securely inside the JVM. Practical: First native compilation and CLI execution.

modules.title: Module 2: Strict Typing & Primitives
modules.content: Deploy Java's rigid variable typing system. Map the exact byte capacities of native primitives (byte, short, int, long, float, double, boolean). Understand implicit widening and explicit narrowing typecasting constraints.

modules.title: Module 3: Control Flow & Operational Logic
modules.content: Master Boolean operators, deep-nested execution trees (if/else), specialized switch expressions, and strictly iterated loops natively. Practical: Deploy an iterative numerical processing tool.

modules.title: Module 4: Primitive Arrays & Iterables
modules.content: Create contiguous blocks of static memory using Arrays. Map execution using enhanced explicit for-loops. Practical task: Write native recursive and iterative sorting architectures (Bubble and Insertion sort matrices).

modules.title: Module 5: Core OOP: Classes & Instantiation
modules.content: Transition from procedural execution into heavy Object constraints. Design Classes natively, build detailed Constructors, deploy `this` keywords securely, and map objects dynamically inside the local Heap.

modules.title: Module 6: Encapsulation & Access Modifiers
modules.content: Enforce extreme data security leveraging `private`, `protected`, and `public` variables identically. Utilize cleanly designed Getter/Setter methods to strictly regulate data ingestion and mutation natively.

modules.title: Module 7: Inheritance & Deep Polymorphism
modules.content: Scale your execution code via `extends`. Understand superclass routing natively. Use Method Overriding and Abstract interfaces securely to construct incredibly modular applications seamlessly.

modules.title: Module 8: String Manipulation & Memory Constraints
modules.content: Understand the String Constant Pool natively. Execute complex text analysis minimizing memory overhead rapidly using the immutable `String` class and scalable `StringBuilder` optimizations.

modules.title: Module 9: The Collections Framework natively
modules.content: Break free of static arrays. Utilize Java's powerful Collections API natively: ArrayList, HashSet, and HashMap natively. Learn structural differences and exact 'Big O' time complexities natively.

modules.title: Module 10: Exception Engineering Final Implementation
modules.content: Handle severe runtime anomalies securely utilizing try-catch-finally workflows and Custom exception mappings natively. Final module: Build an Inventory Management System persisting heavily through structured data handling.

courses.title: Nmap: Practical Network Enumeration
courses.description: Nmap is the undisputed industry standard for network discovery and security auditing. You will learn to execute precise network sweeps, unmask hidden service profiles rapidly, and securely verify vulnerabilities across live nodes.

modules.title: Module 1: TCP/IP Mapping & Installation Protocols
modules.content: Before deploying Nmap, you must comprehend targeted packets. Understand the TCP 3-Way Handshake conceptually. Activity: Install strictly the latest Nmap build across your local testing execution environment.

modules.title: Module 2: Ping Sweeps & Live Target Acquisition
modules.content: Execute fast ICMP sweep architectures (`-sn`) securely to identify active operational nodes without heavily port-scanning them. Understand exactly how Nmap handles local ARP requests dynamically vs external ICMP blocks.

modules.title: Module 3: Basic TCP/UDP Port Scanning
modules.content: Map precise target nodes cleanly using the TCP Connect scan (`-sT`) and the UDP scan (`-sU`). Learn to read standard port state outputs properly (open, closed, filtered natively, open|filtered).

modules.title: Module 4: Stealth Syn execution Operations
modules.content: Deploy the SYN Half-Open scan (`-sS`). This technique requests a connection natively but drops the packet before logging triggers seamlessly. Learn why this requires local root/admin access dynamically.

modules.title: Module 5: Explicit Version & Service Enumeration
modules.content: A port is useless without context. Deploy (`-sV`) natively to deeply interrogate execution banners dynamically mapped to ports. Understand specific service version endpoints reliably.

modules.title: Module 6: Aggressive OS Fingerprinting
modules.content: Execute precise Operation System discovery requests using (`-O`). Understand how Nmap analyzes TCP/IP specific stack behavioral anomalies dynamically to guess the target kernel seamlessly.

modules.title: Module 7: Optimization & Timing Templates
modules.content: Scale execution speed securely. Understand structural Timing blocks (`-T0` through `-T5`). Practical: Design execution sweeps prioritizing stealth over immediate velocity dynamically.

modules.title: Module 8: Output Formats & Greppable Execution
modules.content: Never rely strictly natively on terminal output. Extract scan pipelines across XML (`-oX`) and Greppable (`-oG`) outputs seamlessly. Practice passing standard Nmap greppable outputs natively into Bash pipelines.

modules.title: Module 9: Nmap Scripting Engine (NSE) Execution
modules.content: Leverage Lua-based automated execution scripts properly. Run structural `--script vuln` parameters securely mapping out deep vulnerability frameworks natively across verified services.

modules.title: Module 10: Advanced Firewall Evasion Operations
modules.content: Pass packets cleanly through explicitly restrictive networking architectures. Learn explicit MAC spoofing (`--spoof-mac`), packet fragmentation operations (`-f`), and native Decoy execution parameters (`-D`).

courses.title: Burp Suite: Web Vulnerability Interception
courses.description: Burp Suite operates as the core execution proxy for manual web application testing. Learn to map, intercept, modify, and replay standard HTTP requests uncovering massive API and endpoint flaws.

modules.title: Module 1: Architecture & Proxy Routing Configuration
modules.content: Grasp the concept of a Man-In-The-Middle Local Proxy securely. Install Burp Suite Community natively and cleanly bridge Firefox (via FoxyProxy) to route all specific HTTP/S packets directly cleanly into your interceptor.

modules.title: Module 2: Trust Ingestion & Interceptor Basics
modules.content: Resolve the 'Site Not Secure' HTTPS collisions smoothly by injecting PortSwigger's local CA Certificate. Practice securely toggling your `Intercept` switch explicitly pausing a live request.

modules.title: Module 3: Mapping the Target Architecture
modules.content: Use the local 'Site Map' and HTTP history logs comprehensively. Learn specifically how explicitly adding an application to the 'Target Scope' removes absolute extraneous noise from your workflow completely.

modules.title: Module 4: Packet Manipulation Execution
modules.content: Pause an outbound authentication request directly natively. Modify execution parameters cleanly (such as altering user-agent models or flipping execution boolean logic cleanly) before cleanly pushing the packet forward securely.

modules.title: Module 5: The Repeater Module Concept
modules.content: Send explicit intercepted packets squarely into the 'Repeater'. Here cleanly execute deep-dive parameter fuzzing natively. Replay exact modified payloads identically examining response anomaly outputs perfectly.

modules.title: Module 6: Automated Intruder Operations
modules.content: Load automated Sniper payload execution logic natively into the Intruder securely. Practical: Perform a localized, completely authorized brute-force operation natively against a specialized testing login parameter.

modules.title: Module 7: Deep Decoding & Payload Generation
modules.content: Leverage the local 'Decoder' mapping deeply nested base64 strings securely, explicit URL encodings natively, and complex hex structures cleanly. Convert malicious payloads specifically past standard firewall rules.

modules.title: Module 8: Web Socket Interception Logic
modules.content: Analyze bi-directional WSS connection flows cleanly. Capture, halt, and cleanly inject manipulated logic commands deeply straight into standard WebSocket architectural frames seamlessly.

modules.title: Module 9: Authentication & Session Exploitation
modules.content: Identify insecure Cookie implementations securely natively. Map JWT executions (JSON Web Tokens) deeply inside intercepted headers, examining specifically whether structural signatures hold securely.

modules.title: Module 10: Comparative Execution & Final Testing
modules.content: Utilize the 'Comparer' execution module mapping byte-by-byte visual differences securely across multiple raw structural responses natively, diagnosing specifically complex blind vulnerabilities rapidly.

courses.title: Gobuster: Rapid Directory Discovery
courses.description: Information gathering is structural. Gobuster is a highly scalable, execution-heavy command-line tool written strictly in Go, designed specifically for rapid DNS, Virtual Host, and deep Directory bruteforcing.

modules.title: Module 1: Go-Architecture Execution & Installation
modules.content: Understand Gobuster's precise concurrency models. Install correctly via native `apt` execution endpoints securely or directly mapping the underlying Go release source architectures securely natively.

modules.title: Module 2: Dictionary Ingestion Constraints
modules.content: Gobuster is utterly useless without highly functional payloads. Understand the native Seclists repository. Practical: Map exactly how localized line-based read executions drive extreme multi-thread operations cleanly.

modules.title: Module 3: Native `dir` Mode Execution
modules.content: Execute your absolute first comprehensive directory brute-force securely. Learn specifically the syntax `gobuster dir -u <URL> -w <wordlist>`. Evaluate structural HTTP status outputs securely (200, 301, 403).

modules.title: Module 4: Filtering Output Anomaly Structures
modules.content: Large lists generate massive noise. Use `-b` to explicitly ignore garbage executions (e.g., `-b 404,401`). Isolate cleanly operational successes natively using deep status filters `-s` securely.

modules.title: Module 5: Explicit File Extension Sweeps
modules.content: Most sensitive backups are severely hidden seamlessly. Inject the extension parameter natively (`-x php,bak,txt,tar.gz`) to force specific file endpoints securely appending explicitly to every single wordlist check logically.

modules.title: Module 6: Deep Virtual Host (VHost) Exploitation
modules.content: Switch to `vhost` operational execution mode securely. Break standard Host header logic natively attempting specifically to expose internal administrative execution domains severely hidden squarely behind reverse proxies.

modules.title: Module 7: Native `dns` Subdomain Discovery Mode
modules.content: Execute precise DNS brute-forcing rapidly targeting massive namespaces cleanly. Differentiate explicitly between traditional DNS structural transfers natively and hard execution dictionary discovery loops.

modules.title: Module 8: Thread Scaling & Rate Limitation Mapping
modules.content: Increase concurrent operational sweeps securely leveraging parameter `-t`. Understand strictly why maxing operational threads cleanly causes highly unstable connection drops securely, necessitating precise tuning natively.

modules.title: Module 9: Stealth & Evasion Execution Protocols
modules.content: Randomize explicit custom User-Agents organically using `-a`. Override explicit request methodologies securely explicitly and cleanly append highly specific headers dynamically bypassing basic WAF (Web Application Firewall) sweeps.

modules.title: Module 10: Deep Automation Command Scripting
modules.content: Pipe structural outputs cleanly. Design highly automated native Bash execution wrappers cleanly pulling live targets squarely from external `.txt` arrays, processing massive automated Gobuster sweeps efficiently seamlessly.
